NKJV.1: The preparations of the heart belong to man, But the answer of the tongue is from the LORD.
NKJV.2: All the ways of a man are pure in his own eyes, But the LORD weighs the spirits.
NKJV.3: Commit your works to the LORD, And your thoughts will be established.
NKJV.4: The LORD has made all for Himself,  Yes, even the wicked for the day of doom.
NKJV.5: Everyone proud in heart is an abomination to the LORD; Though they join forces, none will go unpunished.
NKJV.6: In mercy and truth Atonement is provided for iniquity; And by the fear of the LORD one departs from evil.
NKJV.7: When a man’s ways please the LORD, He makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.
NKJV.8: Better is a little with righteousness, Than vast revenues without justice.
NKJV.9: A man’s heart plans his way,  But the LORD directs his steps.
NKJV.10: Divination is on the lips of the king; His mouth must not transgress in judgment.
NKJV.11: Honest weights and scales are the LORD’s; All the weights in the bag are His work.
NKJV.12: It is an abomination for kings to commit wickedness, For a throne is established by righteousness.
NKJV.13: Righteous lips are the delight of kings, And they love him who speaks what is right.
NKJV.14: As messengers of death is the king’s wrath, But a wise man will appease it.
NKJV.15: In the light of the king’s face is life, And his favor is like a cloud of the latter rain.
NKJV.16: How much better to get wisdom than gold! And to get understanding is to be chosen rather than silver.
NKJV.17: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil; He who keeps his way preserves his soul.
NKJV.18: Pride goes before destruction, And a haughty spirit before a fall.
NKJV.19: Better to be of a humble spirit with the lowly, Than to divide the spoil with the proud.
NKJV.20: He who heeds the word wisely will find good, And whoever trusts in the LORD, happy is he.
NKJV.21: The wise in heart will be called prudent, And sweetness of the lips increases learning.
NKJV.22: Understanding is a wellspring of life to him who has it. But the correction of fools is folly.
NKJV.23: The heart of the wise teaches his mouth, And adds learning to his lips.
NKJV.24: Pleasant words are like a honeycomb, Sweetness to the soul and health to the bones.
NKJV.25: There is a way that seems right to a man, But its end is the way of death.
NKJV.26: The person who labors, labors for himself, For his hungry mouth drives him on.
NKJV.27: An ungodly man digs up evil, And it is on his lips like a burning fire.
NKJV.28: A perverse man sows strife, And a whisperer separates the best of friends.
NKJV.29: A violent man entices his neighbor, And leads him in a way that is not good.
NKJV.30: He winks his eye to devise perverse things; He purses his lips and brings about evil.
NKJV.31: The silver-haired head is a crown of glory, If it is found in the way of righteousness.
NKJV.32: He who is slow to anger is better than the mighty, And he who rules his spirit than he who takes a city.
NKJV.33: The lot is cast into the lap, But its every decision is from the LORD.

ESV.1: The plans of the heart belong to man, but the answer of the tongue is from the LORD.
ESV.2: All the ways of a man are pure in his own eyes, but the LORD weighs the spirit.
ESV.3: Commit your work to the LORD, and your plans will be established.
ESV.4: The LORD has made everything for its purpose, even the wicked for the day of trouble.
ESV.5: Everyone who is arrogant in heart is an abomination to the LORD; be assured, he will not go unpunished.
ESV.6: By steadfast love and faithfulness iniquity is atoned for, and by the fear of the LORD one turns away from evil.
ESV.7: When a man's ways please the LORD, he makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.
ESV.8: Better is a little with righteousness than great revenues with injustice.
ESV.9: The heart of man plans his way, but the LORD establishes his steps.
ESV.10: An oracle is on the lips of a king; his mouth does not sin in judgment.
ESV.11: A just balance and scales are the LORD's; all the weights in the bag are his work.
ESV.12: It is an abomination to kings to do evil, for the throne is established by righteousness.
ESV.13: Righteous lips are the delight of a king, and he loves him who speaks what is right.
ESV.14: A king's wrath is a messenger of death, and a wise man will appease it.
ESV.15: In the light of a king's face there is life, and his favor is like the clouds that bring the spring rain.
ESV.16: How much better to get wisdom than gold! To get understanding is to be chosen rather than silver.
ESV.17: The highway of the upright turns aside from evil; whoever guards his way preserves his life.
ESV.18: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
ESV.19: It is better to be of a lowly spirit with the poor than to divide the spoil with the proud.
ESV.20: Whoever gives thought to the word  will discover good, and blessed is he who trusts in the LORD.
ESV.21: The wise of heart is called discerning, and sweetness of speech increases persuasiveness.
ESV.22: Good sense is a fountain of life to him who has it, but the instruction of fools is folly.
ESV.23: The heart of the wise makes his speech judicious and adds persuasiveness to his lips.
ESV.24: Gracious words are like a honeycomb, sweetness to the soul and health to the body.
ESV.25: There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end is the way to death.
ESV.26: A worker's appetite works for him; his mouth urges him on.
ESV.27: A worthless man plots evil, and his speech  is like a scorching fire.
ESV.28: A dishonest man spreads strife, and a whisperer separates close friends.
ESV.29: A man of violence entices his neighbor and leads him in a way that is not good.
ESV.30: Whoever winks his eyes plans  dishonest things; he who purses his lips brings evil to pass.
ESV.31: Gray hair is a crown of glory; it is gained in a righteous life.
ESV.32: Whoever is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and he who rules his spirit than he who takes a city.
ESV.33: The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from the LORD.

NASB.1: The plans of the heart belong to man, But the answer of the tongue is from the LORD.
NASB.2: All the ways of a man are clean in his own sight, But the LORD weighs the motives.
NASB.3: Commit your works to the LORD And your plans will be established.
NASB.4: The LORD has made everything for its own purpose, Even the wicked for the day of evil.
NASB.5: Everyone who is proud in heart is an abomination to the LORD; Assuredly, he will not be unpunished.
NASB.6: By lovingkindness and truth iniquity is atoned for, And by the fear of the LORD one keeps away from evil.
NASB.7: When a man's ways are pleasing to the LORD, He makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.
NASB.8: Better is a little with righteousness Than great income with injustice.
NASB.9: The mind of man plans his way, But the LORD directs his steps.
NASB.10: A divine decision is in the lips of the king; His mouth should not err in judgment.
NASB.11: A just balance and scales belong to the LORD; All the weights of the bag are His concern.
NASB.12: It is an abomination for kings to commit wicked acts, For a throne is established on righteousness.
NASB.13: Righteous lips are the delight of kings, And he who speaks right is loved.
NASB.14: The fury of a king is [like] messengers of death, But a wise man will appease it.
NASB.15: In the light of a king's face is life, And his favor is like a cloud with the spring rain.
NASB.16: How much better it is to get wisdom than gold! And to get understanding is to be chosen above silver.
NASB.17: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil; He who watches his way preserves his life.
NASB.18: Pride [goes] before destruction, And a haughty spirit before stumbling.
NASB.19: It is better to be humble in spirit with the lowly Than to divide the spoil with the proud.
NASB.20: He who gives attention to the word will find good, And blessed is he who trusts in the LORD.
NASB.21: The wise in heart will be called understanding, And sweetness of speech increases persuasiveness.
NASB.22: Understanding is a fountain of life to one who has it, But the discipline of fools is folly.
NASB.23: The heart of the wise instructs his mouth And adds persuasiveness to his lips.
NASB.24: Pleasant words are a honeycomb, Sweet to the soul and healing to the bones.
NASB.25: There is a way [which seems] right to a man, But its end is the way of death.
NASB.26: A worker's appetite works for him, For his hunger urges him [on].
NASB.27: A worthless man digs up evil, While his words are like scorching fire.
NASB.28: A perverse man spreads strife, And a slanderer separates intimate friends.
NASB.29: A man of violence entices his neighbor And leads him in a way that is not good.
NASB.30: He who winks his eyes [does so] to devise perverse things; He who compresses his lips brings evil to pass.
NASB.31: A gray head is a crown of glory; It is found in the way of righteousness.
NASB.32: He who is slow to anger is better than the mighty, And he who rules his spirit, than he who captures a city.
NASB.33: The lot is cast into the lap, But its every decision is from the LORD.

NLT.1:
We can make our own plans,
but the LORD gives the right answer.
NLT.2:
People may be pure in their own eyes,
but the LORD examines their motives.
NLT.3:
Commit your actions to the LORD,
and your plans will succeed.
NLT.4:
The LORD has made everything for his own purposes,
even the wicked for a day of disaster.
NLT.5:
The LORD detests the proud;
they will surely be punished.
NLT.6:
Unfailing love and faithfulness make atonement for sin.
By fearing the LORD, people avoid evil.
NLT.7:
When people’s lives please the LORD,
even their enemies are at peace with them.
NLT.8:
Better to have little, with godliness,
than to be rich and dishonest.
NLT.9:
We can make our plans,
but the LORD determines our steps.
NLT.10:
The king speaks with divine wisdom;
he must never judge unfairly.
NLT.11:
The LORD demands accurate scales and balances;
he sets the standards for fairness.
NLT.12:
A king detests wrongdoing,
for his rule is built on justice.
NLT.13:
The king is pleased with words from righteous lips;
he loves those who speak honestly.
NLT.14:
The anger of the king is a deadly threat;
the wise will try to appease it.
NLT.15:
When the king smiles, there is life;
his favor refreshes like a spring rain.
NLT.16:
How much better to get wisdom than gold,
and good judgment than silver!
NLT.17:
The path of the virtuous leads away from evil;
whoever follows that path is safe.
NLT.18:
Pride goes before destruction,
and haughtiness before a fall.
NLT.19:
Better to live humbly with the poor
than to share plunder with the proud.
NLT.20:
Those who listen to instruction will prosper;
those who trust the LORD will be joyful.
NLT.21:
The wise are known for their understanding,
and pleasant words are persuasive.
NLT.22:
Discretion is a life-giving fountain to those who possess it,
but discipline is wasted on fools.
NLT.23:
From a wise mind comes wise speech;
the words of the wise are persuasive.
NLT.24:
Kind words are like honey —
sweet to the soul and healthy for the body.
NLT.25:
There is a path before each person that seems right,
but it ends in death.
NLT.26:
It is good for workers to have an appetite;
an empty stomach drives them on.
NLT.27:
Scoundrels create trouble;
their words are a destructive blaze.
NLT.28:
A troublemaker plants seeds of strife;
gossip separates the best of friends.
NLT.29:
Violent people mislead their companions,
leading them down a harmful path.
NLT.30:
With narrowed eyes, people plot evil;
with a smirk, they plan their mischief.
NLT.31:
Gray hair is a crown of glory;
it is gained by living a godly life.
NLT.32:
Better to be patient than powerful;
better to have self-control than to conquer a city.
NLT.33:
We may throw the dice,
but the LORD determines how they fall.

WEB.1: The plans of the heart belong to man, but the answer of the tongue is from Yahweh.
WEB.2: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but Yahweh weighs the motives.
WEB.3: Commit your deeds to Yahweh, and your plans shall succeed.
WEB.4: Yahweh has made everything for its own end — yes, even the wicked for the day of evil.
WEB.5: Everyone who is proud in heart is an abomination to Yahweh: they shall certainly not be unpunished.
WEB.6: By mercy and truth iniquity is atoned for. By the fear of Yahweh men depart from evil.
WEB.7: When a man’s ways please Yahweh, he makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.
WEB.8: Better is a little with righteousness, than great revenues with injustice.
WEB.9: A man’s heart plans his course, but Yahweh directs his steps.
WEB.10: Inspired judgments are on the lips of the king. He shall not betray his mouth.
WEB.11: Honest balances and scales are Yahweh’s; all the weights in the bag are his work.
WEB.12: It is an abomination for kings to do wrong, for the throne is established by righteousness.
WEB.13: Righteous lips are the delight of kings. They value one who speaks the truth.
WEB.14: The king’s wrath is a messenger of death, but a wise man will pacify it.
WEB.15: In the light of the king’s face is life. His favor is like a cloud of the spring rain.
WEB.16: How much better it is to get wisdom than gold! Yes, to get understanding is to be chosen rather than silver.
WEB.17: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil. He who keeps his way preserves his soul.
WEB.18: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
WEB.19: It is better to be of a lowly spirit with the poor, than to divide the plunder with the proud.
WEB.20: He who heeds the Word finds prosperity. Whoever trusts in Yahweh is blessed.
WEB.21: The wise in heart shall be called prudent. Pleasantness of the lips promotes instruction.
WEB.22: Understanding is a fountain of life to one who has it, but the punishment of fools is their folly.
WEB.23: The heart of the wise instructs his mouth, and adds learning to his lips.
WEB.24: Pleasant words are a honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.
WEB.25: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.
WEB.26: The appetite of the laboring man labors for him; for his mouth urges him on.
WEB.27: A worthless man devises mischief. His speech is like a scorching fire.
WEB.28: A perverse man stirs up strife. A whisperer separates close friends.
WEB.29: A man of violence entices his neighbor, and leads him in a way that is not good.
WEB.30: One who winks his eyes to plot perversities, one who compresses his lips, is bent on evil.
WEB.31: Gray hair is a crown of glory. It is attained by a life of righteousness.
WEB.32: One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty; one who rules his spirit, than he who takes a city.
WEB.33: The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from Yahweh.

but the answer of the tongue is from the LORD. ⓖ

CSB17.7:

God’s verdict is on the lips of a king; [3] ⓓ

CSB17.11:

all the weights in the bag are his concern. ⓕ
CSB17.12:
Wicked behavior is detestable to kings, ⓖ

CSB17.14:

And get understanding —

CSB17.17:

and an arrogant spirit before a fall. ⓜ

CSB17.23:

it adds learning to his speech. [6] ⓕ
CSB17.24:
Pleasant words are a honeycomb: ⓖ
sweet to the taste [7] and health to the body. [8] ⓗ
CSB17.25:
There is a way that seems right to a person,
but its end is the way to death. ⓘ
CSB17.26:
A worker’s appetite works for him
because his hunger [9] urges him on.
CSB17.27:
A worthless person digs up evil,
and his speech is like a scorching fire. ⓙ
CSB17.28:
A contrary person spreads conflict,
and a gossip separates close friends. ⓚ
CSB17.29:
A violent person lures his neighbor, ⓛ
leading him on a path that is not good. ⓜ
CSB17.30:
The one who narrows his eyes is planning deceptions;
the one who compresses his lips brings about evil. ⓝ
CSB17.31:
Gray hair is a glorious crown; ⓐ
it is found in the ways of righteousness. ⓑ
CSB17.32:
Patience is better than power,
and controlling one’s emotions, [10] than capturing a city. ⓒ
CSB17.33:
The lot is cast into the lap,
but its every decision is from the LORD. ⓓ

AMP.1:
The plans and reflections of the heart belong to man,
But the [wise] answer of the tongue is from the LORD.
AMP.2:
All the ways of a man are clean and innocent in his own eyes [and he may see nothing wrong with his actions],
But the LORD weighs and examines the motives and intents [of the heart and knows the truth].
AMP.3:
Commit your works to the LORD [submit and trust them to Him],
And your plans will succeed [if you respond to His will and guidance].
AMP.4:
The LORD has made everything for its own purpose,
Even the wicked [according to their role] for the day of evil.
AMP.5:
Everyone who is proud and arrogant in heart is disgusting and exceedingly offensive to the LORD;
Be assured he will not go unpunished.
AMP.6:
By mercy and lovingkindness and truth [not superficial ritual] wickedness is cleansed from the heart,
And by the fear of the LORD one avoids evil.
AMP.7:
When a man's ways please the LORD,
He makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.
AMP.8:
Better is a little with righteousness
Than great income [gained] with injustice.
AMP.9:
A man's mind plans his way [as he journeys through life],
But the LORD directs his steps and establishes them.
AMP.10:
A divine decision [given by God] is on the lips of the king [as His representative];
His mouth should not be unfaithful or unjust in judgment.
AMP.11:
A just balance and [honest] scales are the Lord's;
All the weights of the bag are His concern [established by His eternal principles].
AMP.12:
It is repulsive [to God and man] for kings to behave wickedly,
For a throne is established on righteousness (right standing with God).
AMP.13:
Righteous lips are the delight of kings,
And he who speaks right is loved.
AMP.14:
The wrath of a king is like a messenger of death,
But a wise man will appease it.
AMP.15:
In the light of the king's face is life,
And his favor is like a cloud bringing the spring rain.
AMP.16:
How much better it is to get wisdom than gold!
And to get understanding is to be chosen above silver.
AMP.17:
The highway of the upright turns away and departs from evil;
He who guards his way protects his life (soul).
AMP.18:
Pride goes before destruction,
And a haughty spirit before a fall.
AMP.19:
It is better to be humble in spirit with the lowly
Than to divide the spoil with the proud (haughty, arrogant).
AMP.20:
He who pays attention to the word [of God] will find good,
And blessed (happy, prosperous, to be admired) is he who trusts [confidently] in the LORD.
AMP.21:
The wise in heart will be called understanding,
And sweet speech increases persuasiveness and learning [in both speaker and listener].
AMP.22:
Understanding (spiritual insight) is a [refreshing and boundless] wellspring of life to those who have it,
But to give instruction and correction to fools is foolishness.
AMP.23:
The heart of the wise instructs his mouth [in wisdom]
And adds persuasiveness to his lips.
AMP.24:
Pleasant words are like a honeycomb,
Sweet and delightful to the soul and healing to the body.
AMP.25:
There is a way which seems right to a man and appears straight before him,
But its end is the way of death.
AMP.26:
The appetite of a worker works for him,
For his hunger urges him on.
AMP.27:
A worthless man devises and digs up evil,
And the words on his lips are like a scorching fire.
AMP.28:
A perverse man spreads strife,
And one who gossips separates intimate friends.
AMP.29:
A violent and exceedingly covetous man entices his neighbor [to sin],
And leads him in a way that is not good.
AMP.30:
He who [slyly] winks his eyes does so to plot perverse things;
And he who compresses his lips [as if in a secret signal] brings evil to pass.
AMP.31:
The silver-haired head is a crown of splendor and glory;
It is found in the way of righteousness.
AMP.32:
He who is slow to anger is better and more honorable than the mighty [soldier],
And he who rules and controls his own spirit, than he who captures a city.
AMP.33:
The lot is cast into the lap,
But its every decision is from the LORD.

MSG.1:
Mortals make elaborate plans,
but God has the last word.
MSG.2:
Humans are satisfied with whatever looks good;
God probes for what is good.
MSG.3:
Put God in charge of your work,
then what you've planned will take place.
MSG.4:
God made everything with a place and purpose;
even the wicked are included—but for judgment.
MSG.5:
God can't stomach arrogance or pretense;
believe me, he'll put those upstarts in their place.
MSG.6:
Guilt is banished through love and truth;
Fear-of-God deflects evil.
MSG.7:
When God approves of your life,
even your enemies will end up shaking your hand.
MSG.8:
Far better to be right and poor
than to be wrong and rich.
MSG.9:
We plan the way we want to live,
but only God makes us able to live it.
MSG.10:
A good leader motivates,
doesn't mislead, doesn't exploit.
MSG.11:
God cares about honesty in the workplace;
your business is his business.
MSG.12:
Good leaders abhor wrongdoing of all kinds;
sound leadership has a moral foundation.
MSG.13:
Good leaders cultivate honest speech;
they love advisors who tell them the truth.
MSG.14:
An intemperate leader wreaks havoc in lives;
you're smart to stay clear of someone like that.
MSG.15:
Good-tempered leaders invigorate lives;
they're like spring rain and sunshine.
MSG.16:
Get wisdom—it's worth more than money;
choose insight over income every time.
MSG.17:
The road of right living bypasses evil;
watch your step and save your life.
MSG.18:
First pride, then the crash—
the bigger the ego, the harder the fall.
MSG.19:
It's better to live humbly among the poor
than to live it up among the rich and famous.
MSG.20:
It pays to take life seriously;
things work out when you trust in God.
MSG.21:
A wise person gets known for insight;
gracious words add to one's reputation.
MSG.22:
True intelligence is a spring of fresh water,
while fools sweat it out the hard way.
MSG.23:
They make a lot of sense, these wise folks;
whenever they speak, their reputation increases.
MSG.24:
Gracious speech is like clover honey—
good taste to the soul, quick energy for the body.
MSG.25:
There's a way that looks harmless enough;
look again—it leads straight to hell.
MSG.26:
Appetite is an incentive to work;
hunger makes you work all the harder.
MSG.27:
Mean people spread mean gossip;
their words smart and burn.
MSG.28:
Troublemakers start fights;
gossips break up friendships.
MSG.29:
Calloused climbers betray their very own friends;
they'd stab their own grandmothers in the back.
MSG.30:
A shifty eye betrays an evil intention;
a clenched jaw signals trouble ahead.
MSG.31:
Gray hair is a mark of distinction,
the award for a God-loyal life.
MSG.32:
Moderation is better than muscle,
self-control better than political power.
MSG.33:
Make your motions and cast your votes,
but God has the final say.

NIV.1:
To man belong the plans of the heart,
but from the Lord comes the reply of the tongue.
NIV.2:
All a man’s ways seem innocent to him,
but motives are weighed by the Lord.
NIV.3:
Commit to the Lord whatever you do,
and your plans will succeed.
NIV.4:
The Lord works out everything for his own ends—
even the wicked for a day of disaster.
NIV.5:
The Lord detests all the proud of heart.
Be sure of this: They will not go unpunished.
NIV.6:
Through love and faithfulness sin is atoned for;
through the fear of the Lord a man avoids evil.
NIV.7:
When a man’s ways are pleasing to the Lord,
he makes even his enemies live at peace with him.
NIV.8:
Better a little with righteousness
than much gain with injustice.
NIV.9:
In his heart a man plans his course,
but the Lord determines his steps.
NIV.10:
The lips of a king speak as an oracle,
and his mouth should not betray justice.
NIV.11:
Honest scales and balances are from the Lord;
all the weights in the bag are of his making.
NIV.12:
Kings detest wrongdoing,
for a throne is established through righteousness.
NIV.13:
Kings take pleasure in honest lips;
they value a man who speaks the truth.
NIV.14:
A king’s wrath is a messenger of death,
but a wise man will appease it.
NIV.15:
When a king’s face brightens, it means life;
his favor is like a rain cloud in spring.
NIV.16:
How much better to get wisdom than gold,
to choose understanding rather than silver!
NIV.17:
The highway of the upright avoids evil;
he who guards his way guards his life.
NIV.18:
Pride goes before destruction,
a haughty spirit before a fall.
NIV.19:
Better to be lowly in spirit and among the oppressed
than to share plunder with the proud.
NIV.20:
Whoever gives heed to instruction prospers,
and blessed is he who trusts in the Lord.
NIV.21:
The wise in heart are called discerning,
and pleasant words promote instruction.
NIV.22:
Understanding is a fountain of life to those who have it,
but folly brings punishment to fools.
NIV.23:
A wise man’s heart guides his mouth,
and his lips promote instruction.
NIV.24:
Pleasant words are a honeycomb,
sweet to the soul and healing to the bones.
NIV.25:
There is a way that seems right to a man,
but in the end it leads to death.
NIV.26:
The laborer’s appetite works for him;
his hunger drives him on.
NIV.27:
A scoundrel plots evil,
and his speech is like a scorching fire.
NIV.28:
A perverse man stirs up dissension,
and a gossip separates close friends.
NIV.29:
A violent man entices his neighbor
and leads him down a path that is not good.
NIV.30:
He who winks with his eye is plotting perversity;
he who purses his lips is bent on evil.
NIV.31:
Gray hair is a crown of splendor;
it is attained by a righteous life.
NIV.32:
Better a patient man than a warrior,
a man who controls his temper than one who takes a city.
NIV.33:
The lot is cast into the lap,
but its every decision is from the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
The plans of the mind belong to mortals,
but the answer of the tongue is from the LORD.
NRSVCE.2:
All one’s ways may be pure in one’s own eyes,
but the LORD weighs the spirit.
NRSVCE.3:
Commit your work to the LORD,
and your plans will be established.
NRSVCE.4:
The LORD has made everything for its purpose,
even the wicked for the day of trouble.
NRSVCE.5:
All those who are arrogant are an abomination to the LORD;
be assured, they will not go unpunished.
NRSVCE.6:
By loyalty and faithfulness iniquity is atoned for,
and by the fear of the LORD one avoids evil.
NRSVCE.7:
When the ways of people please the LORD,
he causes even their enemies to be at peace with them.
NRSVCE.8:
Better is a little with righteousness
than large income with injustice.
NRSVCE.9:
The human mind plans the way,
but the LORD directs the steps.
NRSVCE.10:
Inspired decisions are on the lips of a king;
his mouth does not sin in judgment.
NRSVCE.11:
Honest balances and scales are the LORD’s;
all the weights in the bag are his work.
NRSVCE.12:
It is an abomination to kings to do evil,
for the throne is established by righteousness.
NRSVCE.13:
Righteous lips are the delight of a king,
and he loves those who speak what is right.
NRSVCE.14:
A king’s wrath is a messenger of death,
and whoever is wise will appease it.
NRSVCE.15:
In the light of a king’s face there is life,
and his favor is like the clouds that bring the spring rain.
NRSVCE.16:
How much better to get wisdom than gold!
To get understanding is to be chosen rather than silver.
NRSVCE.17:
The highway of the upright avoids evil;
those who guard their way preserve their lives.
NRSVCE.18:
Pride goes before destruction,
and a haughty spirit before a fall.
NRSVCE.19:
It is better to be of a lowly spirit among the poor
than to divide the spoil with the proud.
NRSVCE.20:
Those who are attentive to a matter will prosper,
and happy are those who trust in the LORD.
NRSVCE.21:
The wise of heart is called perceptive,
and pleasant speech increases persuasiveness.
NRSVCE.22:
Wisdom is a fountain of life to one who has it,
but folly is the punishment of fools.
NRSVCE.23:
The mind of the wise makes their speech judicious,
and adds persuasiveness to their lips.
NRSVCE.24:
Pleasant words are like a honeycomb,
sweetness to the soul and health to the body.
NRSVCE.25:
Sometimes there is a way that seems to be right,
but in the end it is the way to death.
NRSVCE.26:
The appetite of workers works for them;
their hunger urges them on.
NRSVCE.27:
Scoundrels concoct evil,
and their speech is like a scorching fire.
NRSVCE.28:
A perverse person spreads strife,
and a whisperer separates close friends.
NRSVCE.29:
The violent entice their neighbors,
and lead them in a way that is not good.
NRSVCE.30:
One who winks the eyes plans perverse things;
one who compresses the lips brings evil to pass.
NRSVCE.31:
Gray hair is a crown of glory;
it is gained in a righteous life.
NRSVCE.32:
One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty,
and one whose temper is controlled than one who captures a city.
NRSVCE.33:
The lot is cast into the lap,
but the decision is the LORD’s alone.

NET.1: The intentions of the heart belong to a man, but the answer of the tongue comes from the LORD.
NET.2: All a person’s ways seem right in his own opinion, but the LORD evaluates the motives.
NET.3: Commit your works to the LORD, and your plans will be established.
NET.4: The LORD works everything for its own ends — even the wicked for the day of disaster.
NET.5: The LORD abhors every arrogant person; rest assured that they will not go unpunished.
NET.6: Through loyal love and truth iniquity is appeased; through fearing the LORD one avoids evil.
NET.7: When a person’s ways are pleasing to the LORD, he even reconciles his enemies to himself.
NET.8: Better to have a little with righteousness than to have abundant income without justice.
NET.9: A person plans his course, but the LORD directs his steps.
NET.10: The divine verdict is in the words of the king, his pronouncements must not act treacherously against justice.
NET.11: Honest scales and balances are from the LORD; all the weights in the bag are his handiwork.
NET.12: Doing wickedness is an abomination to kings, because a throne is established in righteousness.
NET.13: The delight of kings is righteous counsel, and they love the one who speaks uprightly.
NET.14: A king’s wrath is like a messenger of death, but a wise person appeases it.
NET.15: In the light of the king’s face there is life, and his favor is like the clouds of the spring rain.
NET.16: How much better it is to acquire wisdom than gold; to acquire understanding is more desirable than silver.
NET.17: The highway of the upright is to turn away from evil; the one who guards his way safeguards his life.
NET.18: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
NET.19: It is better to be lowly in spirit with the afflicted than to share the spoils with the proud.
NET.20: The one who deals wisely in a matter will find success, and blessed is the one who trusts in the LORD.
NET.21: The one who is wise in heart is called discerning, and kind speech increases persuasiveness.
NET.22: Insight is like a life-giving fountain to the one who possesses it, but folly leads to the discipline of fools.
NET.23: A wise person’s heart makes his speech wise and it adds persuasiveness to his words.
NET.24: Pleasant words are like a honeycomb, sweet to the soul and healing to the bones.
NET.25: There is a way that seems right to a person, but its end is the way that leads to death.
NET.26: A laborer’s appetite works on his behalf, for his hunger urges him to work.
NET.27: A wicked scoundrel digs up evil, and his slander is like a scorching fire.
NET.28: A perverse person spreads dissension, and a gossip separates the closest friends.
NET.29: A violent person entices his neighbor, and leads him down a path that is terrible.
NET.30: The one who winks his eyes devises perverse things, and one who compresses his lips brings about evil.
NET.31: Gray hair is like a crown of glory; it is attained in the path of righteousness.
NET.32: Better to be slow to anger than to be a mighty warrior, and one who controls his temper is better than one who captures a city.
NET.33: The dice are thrown into the lap, but their every decision is from the LORD.
